docs.accessibilityDesc: PaperPost treats accessibility as a foundational product capability. It has two dimensions: i18n (internationalization, enabling users of different languages and writing systems to participate equally) and a11y (accessibility, enabling users of different abilities and usage scenarios to read smoothly).
docs.a11yI18nRuleOne: Multi-language coverage: prioritize languages with larger global user populations, while supporting important regional languages and different writing systems.
docs.a11yI18nRuleTwo: Full RTL support: RTL languages like Arabic and Urdu are fully mirrored — from page direction and navigation position to information flow lines — with no mixed-layout chaos.
docs.a11yI18nRuleThree: Writing system adaptation: line breaking and truncation for space-less languages like Chinese, Japanese, Thai; line height and clarity for tonal languages like Vietnamese and Thai are individually optimized.
docs.a11yA11yRuleOne: Font size & contrast: global font scaling, body text contrast meets WCAG AA, key actions meet AAA, color-blind friendly palette.
docs.a11yA11yRuleTwo: Keyboard & screen reader: full keyboard navigation, visible focus rings, semantic HTML structure, accurate ARIA labels, smooth screen reader narration.
docs.a11yA11yRuleThree: Motion control: respects system 'reduced motion' preference, decorative animations can be turned off, key feedback provides both text and visual channels.
docs.refDesc: Follows W3C Internationalization and Web Accessibility standards to ensure correct implementation of internationalization and accessibility design.
docs.aiAnswerText: The real novelty here lies in expressing reasoning gains as a predictable function.
docs.aiCopy: An AI response separates answer, evidence, and actions into three layers instead of one large chat blob.
docs.aiRuleOne: Lead with a short judgment before longer detail.
docs.aiRuleThree: Give next analysis actions right after the answer.
docs.aiRuleTwo: Quoted evidence and model inference must be visually separated.
docs.calloutCitation: [1] Citation from the original experiment setup section.
docs.calloutInfo: Info: this finding applies from 10B to 100B parameters.
docs.calloutTip: Tip: add it directly to this week’s reading queue.
docs.calloutWarning: Warning: extrapolating from small samples needs care.
docs.commandCopy: Desktop should not stop at a search box. It should have a command entry for navigation, search, and AI actions.
docs.commandRuleOne: Merge search, navigation, and actions into one entry.
docs.commandRuleTwo: Empty input should still show suggested actions.
docs.copilotAnswerCopy: The center area is for reasoning and analysis, not for stuffing citations, related papers, and actions into one bubble.
docs.heroCopy: PaperPost is built for users who need to track cutting-edge papers, conference news, and research trends every day, organizing complex academic information into a clear, trustworthy, and sustainably browsable information experience. Precise recommendations, practical AI, and smooth interactions make cutting-edge research as natural, restrained, and readable as a well-curated knowledge magazine.
docs.iconsDesc: PaperPost uses Phosphor Icons as its cross-platform icon library. Common icons are shown by default; search by keyword and click any icon to copy its Web class name.
docs.iconsRuleFour: Searchable: The official site phosphoricons.com provides icon search. Find the icon you need by keyword and copy platform-specific code.
docs.iconsRuleOne: Phosphor Icons: An open-source, style-consistent icon library with 6 weights (thin / light / regular / bold / fill / duotone). PaperPost uses the regular weight by default.
docs.iconsRuleThree: Outline first: Use outline (regular) icons by default. Only use fill versions for emphasis or specific states to avoid visual clutter.
docs.iconsRuleTwo: Cross-platform consistency: phosphor-web for Web, phosphor-react-native for React Native, PhosphorKit for iOS, Phosphor-Android for Android — all from the same design source files.
docs.miniAuthHeroDesc: Sign in to sync bookmarks, reading progress, keyword preferences, and recommendation feedback. Efficient organization on desktop, effortless reading on mobile.
docs.miniAuthHeroTitle: Tune the paper recommendation system to your research taste.
docs.miniAuthLoginSubtitle: Continue with your bookmarks, recommendation profile, and reading progress.
docs.miniBriefingDesc: Based on 4 interest areas and 3 voting calibrations, here's a tangible starting point for you.
docs.miniBriefingDesc1: Read literature continuously like a researcher, not starting from scratch every time.
docs.miniBriefingDesc2: Tool-calling agents significantly improve review quality.
docs.miniDesktopFeedDesc: The improvement pattern of model reasoning ability becomes predictable, changing how training budgets are planned.
docs.miniDesktopWhyMattersDesc: Key findings, related papers, and next steps all in the right column.
docs.miniFeedDesc1: Precise control of therapeutic switches improves clinical safety.
docs.miniFeedDesc2: The improvement pattern of model reasoning ability becomes estimable.
docs.miniInterestsDesc: No need to be too specific—the system will keep adjusting later. Keep "not interested" to continue refining.
docs.miniInterestsTitle: Pick a few areas you care about
docs.miniLoginSubtitle: Continue with your daily paper briefing, bookmarks, and reading queue.
docs.miniMacosKeyFindingDesc: Reasoning ability improves predictably with compute.
docs.miniNotificationsAuthorDesc: Get notified when followed authors publish new papers.
docs.miniNotificationsDailyDesc: Every morning at 8, 5 curated papers + summaries.
docs.miniNotificationsDesc: Only notify when truly valuable: daily briefing, followed author updates, and key breakthrough pushes. Easy to turn off.
docs.miniNotificationsTitle: Let notifications be a helper, not an interruption.
docs.miniNotificationsTrendDesc: Get notified when important breakthroughs appear in a field.
docs.miniRegisterDesc: We only ask for necessary information.
docs.miniSavedDesc1: This paper provides a theoretical framework for the magneto-optical characterization of superparamagnetic nanoparticles in solution.
docs.miniSavedDesc2: Achieved a 10 cm ultra-stable silicon cavity with extremely low frequency instability at 5K temperature.
docs.miniSurveyDesc: Quickly mark your research interest directions.
docs.miniSurveyPaperDesc: Read literature continuously like a researcher.
docs.miniWelcomeDesc: 5 to 12 high-quality papers every day. First get the key takeaways in plain language, then get the original text, authors, and verifiable sources.
docs.miniWelcomePaperDesc: More → reasoning. Agent and summary quality also improving.
docs.miniWelcomeTitle: Make papers as readable as a feed, but still reliable
docs.pagesCopilotCopy: The AI workspace should feel like a productivity tool, not a generic chat page.
docs.pagesDetailCopy: The upper half of a detail page answers four things only: what it says, the finding, why it matters, and what to do next.
docs.pagesFeedCopy: The home page should start as a scrollable feed, not a filter-heavy backend.
docs.pagesOnboardingCopy: Registration and taste setup should collect only what is necessary instead of asking for the entire profile upfront.
docs.pagesSubtitle: Click a card to view the full page mock. Covers feed, reader, dashboard, onboarding and more.
docs.paperCardCopy: One card should answer whether it is worth reading, saving, and why it was recommended.
docs.paperCardDemoCopy: The summary should feel like a content product, not a database field.
docs.paperCardDemoSource: ArXiv AI · Because you saved reasoning
docs.paperCardRuleOne: Title up to 2 lines, summary up to 3 lines.
docs.paperCardRuleThree: Save, read, and more-like-this should respond in place.
docs.paperCardRuleTwo: Keep recommendation reason in the source line instead of making it a second headline.
docs.philosophyDesc: PaperPost is built for users who need to track cutting-edge papers, conference news, and research trends every day, organizing complex academic information into a clear, trustworthy, and sustainably browsable information experience.
docs.radiusDesc: Rounded corners are the core source of PaperPost Design's approachability. Soft curves counterbalance the seriousness of academic content, making the consumption experience of research information lighter and more friendly.
docs.radiusRuleOne: Approachability first: Large rounded corners are the visual DNA of PaperPost, using soft curves to build an approachable, agile, non-sharp product personality.
docs.radiusRuleTwo: Hierarchy correspondence: The more prominent and interactive the element, the larger the radius; container elements have moderate rounded corners, forming a clear visual hierarchy.
docs.refBidiDesc: Handling RTL and bidirectional text in HTML/CSS
docs.refDesc: Following W3C Internationalization and accessibility-related specifications to ensure correct implementation of multilingual and accessible design.
docs.refLinebreakDesc: Line breaking and typography differences across writing systems
docs.refRtlDesc: Basic text direction setting for right-to-left scripts
docs.ruleOneCopy: Components default to quick judgment, not academic display. Each card carries one main decision: to read or not.
docs.ruleThreeCopy: Let users discover value continuously, not through noise and stimulation. Light feedback, clear rhythm, each paper leads to the next.
docs.ruleTwoCopy: No Times New Roman or academic serif styling. Build credibility through clear hierarchy, evidence chains, and complete sources.
docs.sheetCopy: On mobile, feedback, filters, and extra actions should use a bottom sheet instead of hiding behind obscure long-press menus.
docs.sheetRuleOne: Put primary actions first and risky actions at the bottom.
docs.sheetRuleThree: Closing motion should feel clean and quick.
docs.sheetRuleTwo: Keep it reachable with one hand without crushing reading space.
docs.summaryBrief: More high-quality data and compute improve reasoning performance steadily.
docs.summaryCopy: Use it in the upper half of a detail page to compress the key point into one glance.
docs.summaryDetail: More high-quality data and more training compute improve reasoning performance steadily, and the trend is predictable enough to inform budgeting and experiment planning.
docs.summaryRuleOne: 2 to 4 lines only. No long paragraphs.
docs.summaryRuleTwo: Highlight the conclusion without heavy saturated blocks.
docs.textActionsDesc: Text tools should feel like editor controls, not marketing buttons. Callouts use light glass surfaces and thin borders instead of saturated blocks.
docs.textActionsRuleOne: Use familiar icons for formatting actions, with hover titles for explanation.
docs.textActionsRuleThree: Citation, Info, Warning, and Tip share one glass label system; semantics come from the icon and left rule.
docs.textActionsRuleTwo: Keep primary actions tactile and reduce contrast for secondary actions.
docs.textToolbarDesc: Text formatting tools should feel like editor controls, not marketing buttons. Use familiar icons and reveal meanings on hover.
docs.textToolbarRuleOne: Use familiar icons for formatting actions, with hover titles for explanation.
docs.textToolbarRuleTwo: Evenly spaced icons with clear grouping to prevent misclicks.
docs.textToolbarRuleThree: Active state uses a light background for feedback, not heavy color blocks.
docs.buttonsCalloutsDesc: Primary buttons keep a solid feel, secondary actions reduce contrast. Callouts use light glass surfaces and thin borders instead of saturated blocks.
docs.buttonsCalloutsRuleOne: Keep primary actions tactile and reduce contrast for secondary actions. Avoid a row of buttons all fighting for attention.
docs.buttonsCalloutsRuleTwo: Consistent icon-to-text spacing and ample click areas.
docs.buttonsCalloutsRuleThree: Citation, Info, Warning, and Tip share one glass label system; semantics come from the icon and left rule.
docs.citationTraceDemoRef: Languages Are Still a Major Barrier to Global Science. PLOS Biology. DOI: 10.1371/journal.pbio.2000933.
docs.citationTraceDemoText: Non-English research is easier to miss in global discovery and review workflows, so cross-language reading is knowledge-access infrastructure rather than only translation.
docs.citationTraceDesc: A traceable citation pattern for paper reading, AI summaries, and data insights: solid inline markers locate sources quickly, matching solid reference numbers keep orientation, and the return control at the end of each entry jumps back to the cited passage.
docs.citationTraceRuleOne: Use a solid circular number directly after the supported data, claim, or judgment; do not give it its own line.
docs.citationTraceRuleThree: Reference entries keep author, year, title, publication or institution, DOI / URL, and place the return control at the end like a footnote back-link.
docs.citationTraceRuleTwo: Clicking the inline marker jumps to the reference and pulses it once; reference numbers use the same solid circle for visual pairing.
docs.switchDemoMotionDesc: Lower decorative motion while preserving essential feedback.
docs.switchDesc: Rounded left-right sliders are used for clear on/off states, suitable for preferences, notifications, and reading assistance.
docs.switchRuleOne: Switches are only for binary states: on/off or enabled/disabled, not multiple choices.
docs.switchRuleTwo: The thumb slides left and right, the track changes color, and state does not rely on color alone.
docs.switchRuleThree: Labels clearly name the controlled setting; disabled states reduce opacity while staying readable.
docs.breadcrumbDesc: Breadcrumb navigation shows the user's current position with clear hierarchy, lightweight and unobtrusive.
docs.breadcrumbRuleOne: Navigation hints should stay light and never steal attention from content.
docs.breadcrumbRuleTwo: Use thin arrow separators, not thick vertical lines.
docs.breadcrumbRuleThree: Current page is bold and not a link.
docs.tooltipDesc: Lightweight tooltip bubble that appears on hover or focus to explain icons or abbreviated information.
docs.tooltipRuleOne: Only short explanations, no long paragraphs.
docs.tooltipRuleTwo: Bubble uses light glass surface with thin border.
docs.tooltipRuleThree: Remains readable in dark mode with sufficient contrast.
docs.menuDesc: Dropdown menu carries more actions, with clear icon+text grouping and separately identified danger actions.
docs.menuRuleOne: Menu items left-align icons and text with consistent spacing.
docs.menuRuleTwo: Danger actions use accent color and sit at the bottom of the menu.
docs.menuRuleThree: Hover state uses light background, not heavy color blocks.
docs.dialogDesc: Modal dialog for confirming important actions, with clear hierarchy of title, description, and action buttons.
docs.dialogRuleOne: Overlay interior maintains clear light or dark surface hierarchy.
docs.dialogRuleTwo: Primary action on the right, secondary on the left, consistent button order.
docs.dialogRuleThree: Readable on both mobile and dark mode.
docs.toastDesc: Lightweight feedback bar that appears briefly after an action to confirm results without interrupting the user flow.
docs.toastRuleOne: Icon + short sentence, moderate information density.
docs.toastRuleTwo: Different semantics use different icon colors, with unified glass background.
docs.toastRuleThree: Auto-dismisses and also supports manual close.
docs.filterPillsDesc: Capsule-shaped filter tags for quickly switching categories, topics, or views.
docs.filterPillsRuleOne: Icon + text, semantics at a glance.
docs.filterPillsRuleTwo: Active state uses light background + stroke, not heavy color blocks.
docs.filterPillsRuleThree: Supports both multi-select and single-select modes.
docs.searchInputDesc: Search input with icon, supporting leading icon and trailing action button.
docs.searchInputRuleOne: Search icon at the front, action button at the end.
docs.searchInputRuleTwo: Helper text below the input, not taking up space.
docs.searchInputRuleThree: Supports mixed-field search: title, author, DOI, tags.
docs.statusIndicatorsDesc: Multi-color status tags conveying sync, success, warning, error, and other semantics.
docs.statusIndicatorsRuleTwo: Four semantic colors: info, success, warning, danger.
docs.statusIndicatorsRuleThree: Glass background + thin border, doesn't steal attention from body text.
docs.readingListDesc: Compact reading list with rank, title, description, and action buttons in one row.
docs.readingListRuleOne: Rank, title, description, actions — four-column structure is clear.
docs.readingListRuleTwo: Read items reduce contrast while keeping information complete.
docs.readingListRuleThree: Action buttons right-aligned, not interfering with reading.
docs.metricsDesc: Data summary cards showing key metric values and meanings.
docs.metricsRuleOne: Label on top, value below, clear hierarchy.
docs.metricsRuleTwo: Metrics separated by thin dividers or whitespace.
docs.metricsRuleThree: Values use heavy font weight, labels use muted color.
docs.noticeBarDesc: Light notice bar for system notifications, daily briefing generation, and other non-blocking information.
docs.noticeBarRuleTwo: Pale yellow background only for emphasis, not as page background.
docs.noticeBarRuleThree: Can be manually closed or auto-dismissed.
docs.typographyDesc: Low-distraction typography is the reading foundation of PaperPost. Restrained type scale, distinct weight contrast, and comfortable line height — letting the paper content itself take center stage, while typography stays as quiet support.
docs.typographyRuleOneDesc: Only four levels — Display / Heading / Body / Caption — avoiding too many font sizes that create visual noise. Large titles establish presence, body text stays quietly readable, auxiliary info doesn't steal attention.
docs.typographyRuleTwoDesc: Headings use 700-800 bold to establish hierarchy, body text uses 400 regular for long-form comfort, auxiliary text uses 500 medium to stay clear. Contrast is clear but never harsh.
docs.typographyRuleThreeDesc: Tight line height for large titles (1.15) for presence, loose for body text (1.60) for reading comfort, moderate for subheadings (1.30-1.35) balancing density and breathing room.
docs.colorsDesc: Neutral and restrained backgrounds, precisely placed brand colors, and semantic colors each with their own role. No colorful atmosphere — content itself is the protagonist. Deep Sea and Ice Aqua only appear where they matter.
docs.colorsRuleOneDesc: The main page background is paper white and light gray — content itself is the protagonist, not colorful atmosphere. The background is just a quiet canvas.
docs.colorsRuleTwoDesc: Deep Sea and Ice Aqua are only used for brand touchpoints and key actions, not covering large areas. They appear with purpose — never overused, never out of balance.
docs.colorsRuleThreeDesc: Moss for suggestions, Ochre for reminders, Proof Red for risks — each has its role, no mixing. Color is signal; using color is communication.
docs.commonUiDesc: Dialogs, tooltips, breadcrumbs, menus, and toasts are the small pieces that decide whether this is a real design system or just a pretty page.
docs.commonUiRuleOne: Navigation hints should stay light and never steal attention from the content.
docs.commonUiRuleTwo: Overlays still need a clear light or dark surface hierarchy instead of large colored fills.
docs.commonUiRuleThree: Tooltips, toasts, and dialogs must stay readable on mobile and in dark mode.
docs.commonTooltipCopy: Distinguish cited evidence from model inference.
docs.commonDialogCopy: The system will rebuild tomorrow's briefing from these four directions and the last seven days of reading behavior.
docs.componentKitDesc: Apply color, type, and state to real controls: filters, inputs, status, lists, metrics, and notices should all feel coherent.
docs.kitInputHelp: Search across title, author, DOI, and topic tags in one field.
docs.kitListItemOne: Clear method contribution, worth adding to this week's review.
docs.kitListItemTwo: Already read, keep as a citation candidate.
docs.kitNoticeCopy: Today's briefing is ready. Pale yellow is for emphasis only, not the page background.
docs.emailDesc: A daily email should read like a clean editor's letter: today's judgment first, then papers, key findings, and actions.
docs.emailHeadline: Five papers worth opening first today
docs.emailIntro: Three trackable signals emerged across AI agents, long-context retrieval, and life-science modeling.
docs.emailPaperOne: Putting tool use and evidence citations into review workflows noticeably reduces omissions.
docs.emailPaperTwo: Long context is not only about window size; retrieval quality still decides reading efficiency.
docs.emailNoteTypeCopy: Use a clean sans body and keep headings bold without drifting into poster scale.
docs.emailNoteColorCopy: White base, soft gray sections, pale yellow highlight bars, and a deep-sea primary button keep it clear in dark inboxes and on mobile.
landing.aiAnswerCopy: The real novelty here lies in expressing reasoning gains as a predictable function.
landing.cardComponentsCopy: Cards, command bar, summary block, AI answers, and bottom sheets all come with real state changes.
landing.cardPagesCopy: Feed, detail page, AI workspace, and onboarding become reusable patterns instead of one-off screens.
landing.cardPagesTitle: Page cases come as reusable templates
landing.cardPhilosophyCopy: No vague storytelling. Just product tone, typography stance, density, and interaction rules.
landing.cardPhilosophyTitle: Keep only the principles that matter
landing.feature1Copy: No need to repeatedly search through massive papers—every day, see high-value content directly related to your research direction, interests, and reading habits.
landing.feature2Copy: AI focuses on distilling paper highlights, explaining methodological contributions, and generating speed-read summaries to help you quickly judge whether a paper is worth reading.
landing.feature3Copy: The interface is restrained, clear, and smooth. Browse, read, save, track—all in one flow, making research information naturally enter your workflow like everyday content consumption.
landing.footerDesc: Precise recommendations, practical AI, and smooth interactions—make cutting-edge research like a well-curated knowledge magazine.
landing.heroSubtitle: With clear, restrained, and trustworthy UI, turn complex cutting-edge knowledge into an information experience that everyone can find, read, and use faster.
landing.kfCopy: The upward trend in reasoning performance becomes estimable, which will change how training budgets and evaluation plans are made.
landing.kfPlainEnglish: Bigger models plus better data steadily improve reasoning ability.
landing.kfWhyItMatters: Teams can plan compute budgets more accurately and reduce blind trial and error.
landing.missionDesc: PaperPost Design System serves a clear mission: make frontier knowledge easier to discover, read, compare, and turn into judgment. As paper volume grows, entry points scatter across platforms, and research writing becomes denser, recommendation, summarization, evidence location, terminology explanation, and cross-language reading are organized as one continuous information path. The system helps researchers find relevant work faster, decide whether a paper deserves deeper reading, and capture its methods, contributions, limits, and next actions.
landing.missionDesc2: PaperPost aims to make complex information clearer instead of making papers look busier. Precise recommendation reduces wasted search, structured reading surfaces hold long-form text and multimodal evidence, AI extracts key findings and verifiable support, and saving, comparison, tracking, and alerts connect a single reading session to a longer research workflow. Multilingual support, RTL, font scaling, and high contrast remain core infrastructure, but they serve a larger goal: lower the threshold for entering the research frontier so more people can participate in understanding, judgment, and creation.
landing.missionDesc3: PaperPost therefore keeps its visual language restrained: less decoration, more structure; fewer one-off summaries, more loops from discovery to reading to action. The design system standardizes more than colors and components. It defines how complex papers are filtered, explained, checked, compared, and reused.
landing.missionMetric1Label: The world now publishes scholarly articles at a multi-million-per-year scale, making discovery itself part of research work.
landing.missionMetric2Label: Modern scientific literature has sustained high growth, so reading systems must help people filter and judge.
landing.missionInsightGrowth: ; modern scientific literature has sustained high growth, so reading systems must help people filter, judge, and track
landing.missionInsightLanguage: . Language is also infrastructure: Amano et al. show that non-English knowledge is easier to miss in global circulation, and 54% of protected-area directors identified language as a core barrier to accessing scientific knowledge
landing.missionInsightLead: The three numbers point to one design problem: scholarly publishing is now at a multi-million scale, making discovery itself part of research work
landing.missionInsightOpenScience: ; UNESCO treats multilingual participation and knowledge accessibility as open-science principles, calling for discovery, reading, and dissemination infrastructure that works across languages
landing.missionMetric3Label: 54% of protected-area directors identified language as a core barrier to accessing scientific literature.
landing.missionReference1: Scale of global scholarly publishing and open access, used to support the multi-million-per-year paper volume.
landing.missionReference2: Growth rates of modern science: A bibliometric analysis based on the number of publications and cited references. Journal of the Association for Information Science and Technology.
landing.missionReference3: Languages Are Still a Major Barrier to Global Science. PLOS Biology. DOI: 10.1371/journal.pbio.2000933.
landing.missionReference4: Recommendation on Open Science, emphasizing multilingual participation, knowledge accessibility, and open-science infrastructure.
landing.missionSource1: STM Open Access Dashboard / STM Report: scale of global scholarly publishing and open access.
landing.missionSource2: Bornmann & Mutz, 2015: growth rates of modern science and scholarly information overload.
landing.missionSource3: Amano et al., PLOS Biology, 2016; UNESCO Recommendation on Open Science, 2021: language barriers, visibility of non-English knowledge, and multilingual open-science infrastructure.
landing.missionTitle: The Mission of PaperPost Design System
landing.pageDescription: PaperPost Design System — A high-quality paper feed for researchers. Precise recommendations, practical AI, and smooth interactions make cutting-edge research as natural and readable as a well-curated knowledge magazine.
landing.pageTitle: PaperPost Design — Paper Daily Design System
landing.previewSummary: Know whether to open it in three seconds and whether to save it in ten.
landing.subtitle: The homepage stays minimal. The docs site carries philosophy, component rules, page cases, and interactive demos.
landing.title: A design guideline site for a paper product, built more like Ant Design than a marketing page.

docs.preview.mobileReaderPlain1: Larger models have better reasoning ability.
docs.preview.mobileReaderPlain3: Data quality matters. Higher-quality data leads to greater reasoning improvements.
docs.preview.mobileReaderPlain4: Has practical implications for planning training budgets and evaluation milestones.
docs.preview.mobileSavedDesc1: This paper provides a theoretical framework and experimental methods for magneto-optical characterization of superparamagnetic nanoparticles in solution, analyzing interference in the Michelson interferometer.
docs.preview.mobileSavedDesc2: Investigating bulk plasmon optical properties in elemental metals through first-principles calculations.
docs.preview.mobileSavedDesc3: Investigating bulk plasmon optical properties in elemental metals through first-principles calculations.

docs.preview.onboardingWelcomeDesc: 5 to 12 high-quality papers every day. First get the key takeaways in plain language, then get the original text, authors, and verifiable sources.

docs.preview.onboardingLoginSubtitleText: Continue with your daily paper briefing, bookmarks, and reading queue.

docs.preview.onboardingInterestsImportHi: You can also import your profile
docs.preview.onboardingInterestsImportDe: Paste arXiv, zotero, categories, authors, or fields

docs.preview.onboardingNotificationsDesc: Only notify when truly valuable: daily briefing, followed author updates, and key breakthrough pushes. Easy to turn off.
docs.preview.onboardingNotificationsDail: Every morning at 8, 5 curated papers + summaries.

docs.preview.onboardingBriefingDesc: Based on 4 interest areas and 3 voting calibrations, here's a tangible starting point for you.
